preamble: The diphthong /ue/ can undergo monophthongization to the close-mid back rounded vowel /o/ through a process of vowel reduction, often involving the backing and rounding of the second vowel element and the absorption of the first.

examples.note: In colloquial and informal registers, particularly in Murcian Spanish, the diphthong /ue/ in 'pues' can reduce to /o/ or /ɔ/.
Reduction of /ue/ to /o/ in 'luego' is attested in some Spanish dialects, such as in Madrid.
The reduction of the diphthong 'ue' to 'o' is attested in Anglo-Norman, as seen in the development of words like 'aiuel' to 'aiol'.
Monophthongization of 'ue' to 'o' in Anglo-Norman, exemplified by 'fiuel' becoming 'fillol'.

sources: Murcian diphthongs (white squares: data from Monroy 2008). Spanish... - ResearchGate
